summary: Iteration 6: full E2E coverage of the 4 new communication features (Messages, Document Comments, Inbox, Announcements). Backend pytest 26/26 PASS covering: messages send/empty/self/invalid-recipient/notification/attachment-valid+invalid/conversations/thread-marks-read/unread-count; comments add/empty/list/no-access-403/owner-notification/delete-author/delete-other-403; inbox shared-only/mark-read/404-if-not-shared; announcements admin-create/empty-rejected/agent-403/list-visible-to-all/notifications-broadcast/delete-admin-only. Frontend Playwright validates: sidebar nav-inbox/nav-messages/nav-announcements visible to everyone, nav-reports/nav-agents admin-only; admin can create announcement via new-announcement-btn -> announcement-title/content/submit; dashboard-announcements banner shows latest announcements (2 banners visible after creating TEST_UI_ANN); /messages page renders conv-list with 2 conversations, opening conv shows msg-thread, msg-input/msg-send work and posted message appears; /inbox page renders inbox-page; /announcements page renders for agents WITHOUT new-announcement-btn (agent-403 enforced UI-side too).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1112 lines and mixes 8 feature areas (auth, users, folders, documents, tags, dashboard, activity, notifications, reports, messages, comments, inbox, announcements). Strongly recommend splitting into domain routers (e.g. routers/messages.py, routers/announcements.py, routers/comments.py, routers/inbox.py) and keeping server.py as the FastAPI assembly + lifespan only. Maintainability is degrading rapidly with each iteration.

POST /api/messages with attachment_doc_id silently drops invalid IDs (intended per spec) but does NOT validate the sender has access to the doc — an agent could attach another user's private doc id (if they somehow learn the id) and send it as metadata. Recommend running _has_access(d, user) before storing the attachment; otherwise it's a minor information-disclosure (only id+title+filename are exposed via the message).

POST /api/messages with attachment_doc_id also does NOT grant the recipient access to that document. The recipient sees attachment metadata in the message but the link likely 404s on download unless the doc is also shared. Consider auto-sharing or showing a clear 'sans accès' badge.

GET /api/messages/conversations uses an aggregation pipeline that does not filter by last_message.is_deleted or by archived peer accounts. If a user is deleted, conversations still show 'Utilisateur' fallback (handled gracefully) but the peer.id is stale.
GET /api/announcements does not paginate (limit 100). Fine for now but worth adding a limit/cursor before this list grows.
POST /api/announcements iterates over ALL active users and inserts notifications one-by-one in a loop (await per user). For >1000 users this will be slow; use insert_many with a list comprehension.
DELETE /api/announcements/{id} does not delete the broadcast notifications it created — stale links remain in users' notification panels. Either cascade-delete or mark them as inactive.
Inbox uses a separate db.inbox_reads collection with (user_id, doc_id) — no compound unique index defined. Add db.inbox_reads.create_index([('user_id', 1), ('doc_id', 1)], unique=True) in startup.

Comment delete endpoint does not check whether commenter still has access to the document (irrelevant for delete, but the listing endpoint relies on _has_access — once a share is revoked, prior comments by that user remain visible to others, which may be desirable but worth documenting).

Frontend Documents page: I could only verify the menu trigger exists (count=1) but the Comments menu item count was 0 in this test run because the admin had 0 documents at that moment. Code review of Documents.jsx confirms the comments-{id} testid is wired correctly. Consider seeding a sample doc in dev to make this path always testable.
404s in console: only /api/auth/me 401s right after logout — expected behavior (AuthContext bootstraps the page), no fix needed.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all targeted selectors and flows green: sidebar visibility per role, announcement create+banner, messages thread send, inbox/announcements pages render, agent role guards UI-side)

test_credentials: admin@mhcged.cg / Admin@2026 (confirmed working). Test agents created and cleaned up: TEST_msg_*@mhcged.cg (backend), uitest_iter6_agent@example.com (frontend, deleted at end).
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create+delete TEST_msg_* and TEST_msg2_* agents per module, plus transient TEST_DOC/TEST_ATT/TEST_INBOX/TEST_PRIV documents and TEST_ANN announcements. UI test left one TEST_UI_ANN announcement on the dashboard banner (admin can delete via UI). All other artifacts cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Communication features (Messages, Comments, Inbox, Announcements) are fully green. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_communication.py (26 tests, ~11s). Frontend selectors verified: nav-inbox, nav-messages, nav-announcements, dashboard-announcements, inbox-page, messages-page, conv-list, conv-{peerId}, msg-thread, msg-input, msg-send, announcements-page, new-announcement-btn (admin only), announcement-title/content/submit, comments-{docId} (in document kebab), comments-list, comment-input, comment-submit, comment-delete-{id}. Admin notification broadcast on announcement create works (verified via test_announcement_creates_notifications_for_users). Message-with-attachment stores attachment as embedded metadata (id, title, original_filename) - recipient access to that doc is NOT enforced server-side (see code review comments). All previous iterations 1-5 still green (no regressions exercised, but unchanged endpoints).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — features work as designed. Only design/code-quality observations are listed in critical_code_review_comments.
